subject: Mathematics
title: How Calculus Resolved Zeno
description: A quick, practical guide to How Calculus Resolved Zeno.

hook.text: For 2,000 years, Zeno's paradoxes had no rigorous answer. Then calculus arrived.

buildup.text: Newton and Leibniz created tools for working with infinitely small quantities—infinitesimals.

discovery.text: Limits formalized what 'approaching' means: getting arbitrarily close without needing to arrive.

twist.text: Even calculus was controversial at first. Bishop Berkeley mocked infinitesimals as 'ghosts of departed quantities.'

climax.text: In the 19th century, Cauchy and Weierstrass made limits fully rigorous, settling the debate for good.

punchline.text: It took 2,000 years, but math finally answered a philosopher.

quiz.question: What mathematical concept finally resolved Zeno's paradoxes?
